channel.title: Health Care
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services exposes doctors’ Social Security numbers
item.description: The exposure is linked to a CMS provider directory data intended to help improve accuracy of insurer networks.

media:content.media:title: A national provider directory database used to help people find Medicare plans inadvertently exposed social security numbers for providers.

item.dc:creator: By Robert King and Madi Alexander
item.dc:contributor: By Robert King and Madi Alexander

item.title: Appeals court approves sweeping abortion pill restrictions, teeing up SCOTUS showdown
item.description: The temporary ruling will curtail access nationwide to a drug used in more than two-thirds of abortions.

media:content.media:title: The federal court sided with Louisiana, which challenged the FDA's regulations allowing the drug to be prescribed by telehealth and distributed by mail.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: Cassidy’s uphill reelection race is getting steeper
item.description: Trump, who dislikes the Louisianan, says the senator’s disloyalty cost his surgeon general pick her nomination.

media:content.media:title: Cassidy said multiple senators were opposed to Casey Means.

item.dc:creator: By Cheyenne Haslett and Carmen Paun
item.dc:contributor: By Cheyenne Haslett and Carmen Paun

item.title: Casey Means: Murkowski sank my nomination
item.description: The surgeon general nominee is a close ally of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

media:content.media:title: Means turned off some GOP senators with equivocal comments about vaccination at her confirmation hearing.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Friedman

item.title: Trump pulls Means' nomination for surgeon general
item.description: Nicole Saphier is the president's new pick for the job.

media:content.media:title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. made an impassioned plea to senators to confirm Casey Means earlier this month.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Friedman and Jacob Wendler
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Friedman and Jacob Wendler

item.title: RFK Jr. is holding up $600M in vaccines for poor countries
item.description: The distributor of the shots said it would cost tens of thousands of lives.

media:content.media:title: Kennedy told senators some African and Asian children are getting shots with dangerous ingredients the U.S. has phased out.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: The groups backing RFK Jr. are running low on cash
item.description: Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy this week blasted the MAHA PAC as a “moral and ethical mess.”

media:content.media:title: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. returns from a recess during a Senate Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions Committee hearing on Capitol Hill in Washington, on April 22, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: ‘Anorexic’ reconciliation bill could mean Planned Parenthood gets re-funded
item.description: GOP leadership wants a narrow party-line bill, but rank-and-file seek to extend block on funds to family planning clinics.

media:content.media:title: A divided GOP could allow the restoration of tens of millions of dollars in Medicaid funding to Planned Parenthood this summer.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Robert King
item.dc:contributor: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Robert King

item.title: Trump moves to reschedule marijuana
item.description: The directive aims to expand access to medical researchers.

media:content.media:title: The Justice Department announced tax relief for marijuana companies.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Friedman

item.title: GOP health care policies have set the swamp on fire
item.description: New disclosures show health industry firms and trade groups are spending more than ever to influence Washington.

media:content.media:title: The record lobbying spending coincides with President Donald Trump's move to convince Congress to turn his first-year agenda on health care into law.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: Trump’s surgeon general pick faces mounting GOP opposition
item.description: Add abortion and psychedelics to the list of reasons many Republicans oppose Casey Means.

media:content.media:title: Casey Means, President Donald Trump's surgeon general pick, is facing growing criticism from key camps within the GOP.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Friedman and Alice Miranda Ollstein
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Friedman and Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: RFK Jr.’s followers will help the GOP in midterms, Kennedy adviser tells POLITICO’s Health Summit
item.description: Calley Means said the health secretary and Trump are working in “lockstep.”

media:content.media:description: Calley Means: MAHA movement 'absolutely' continues to fit into Republican Party
media:content.media:title: Calley Means: MAHA movement 'absolutely' continues to fit into Republican Party

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: Mark Cuban is moving on from Kamala Harris
item.description: The billionaire investor who supported the former vice president in 2024 isn't planning to in 2028.

media:content.media:description: Mark Cuban: Kamala Harris should not run for president again
media:content.media:title: Mark Cuban: Kamala Harris should not run for president again

item.dc:creator: By Lauren Gardner

item.title: Top drug lobbyist calls Trump’s efforts to codify his drug pricing deals 'the wrong policy prescription'
item.description: PhRMA CEO Stephen Ubl applauded the Trump administration for pressuring other countries to pay more, is against efforts to make its “most-favored-nation” agreements permanent

media:content.media:description: PhRMA CEO says Most-Favored-Nation drug pricing is 'the wrong prescription'
media:content.media:title: PhRMA CEO says Most-Favored-Nation drug pricing is 'the wrong prescription'

item.dc:creator: By David Lim

item.title: Health care leaders sweat federal Medicaid, Obamacare changes at POLITICO Summit
item.description: “At some point or another, folks are going to lose coverage.”

media:content.media:description: States are scrambling and hospitals are bracing as Medicaid’s work requirements are set to take effect.
media:content.media:title: Virginia Health Secretary: 'Folks are going to lose coverage' under work requirements

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: Oz unveils nationwide plan to tackle Medicaid fraud at POLITICO Summit
item.description: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services will have to audit states “more aggressively” if they don't take the request seriously, Administrator Mehmet Oz said.

media:content.media:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz talks affordability crisis and announces plan to tackle Medicaid fraud
media:content.media:title: Dr. Mehmet Oz talks affordability crisis and announces plan to tackle Medicaid fraud

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: Markey at POLITICO Summit: Ready for ‘battle’ with Trump on health care
item.description: The Massachusetts Democrat also called on Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to resign.

media:content.media:description: Sen. Ed Markey: Trump 'ticks me off every single morning'
media:content.media:title: Sen. Ed Markey: Trump 'ticks me off every single morning'

item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper

item.title: Jayapal at POLITICO Summit: Medicare for All has never been more popular
item.description: The progressive pointed to Cuba's system, before the oil embargo, as superior to America's.

media:content.media:description: Jayapal said swing district voters are now on board with universal health care.
media:content.media:title: Rep. Pramila Jayapal: Medicare for all popularity rising in swing districts

item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien

item.title: Set on ousting Cassidy, MAHA faces first midterms test
item.description: Movement leaders' efforts to sway the midterms, starting with defeating Sen. Bill Cassidy, will help determine whether MAHA outlasts Trump

media:content.media:title: A man holds a hat reading "Make America Healthy Again" at a press conference at the Department of Health and Human Services in Washington, on April 22, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Liz Crampton
item.dc:contributor: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Liz Crampton

item.title: Now it’s Republicans blaming American companies for inflation
item.description: Trump Cabinet members RFK Jr. and Brooke Rollins joined GOP lawmakers Thursday in pillorying onetime allies.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Claudine Hellmuth/POLITICO (source images via AP and Getty)
media:content.media:title: Main.Cabinet-Crossteams.jpg

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun, Rachel Shin and Kelsey Tamborrino
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun, Rachel Shin and Kelsey Tamborrino

item.title: Trump chooses establishment pick to head CDC
item.description: The president selected Erica Schwartz, who served as deputy surgeon general during his first administration.

media:content.media:credit: Department of Health and Human Services via Wikimedia Commons
media:content.media:title: Erica Schwartz is considered a more traditional pick for the CDC director role and does not have clear connections to the Make America Healthy Again movement.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Simon J. Levien
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Simon J. Levien

item.title: ‘He’s got a lot of stamina’: What to watch as the RFK Jr. hearing palooza kicks off
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. will make the case for the Trump budget. Democrats will test his political acumen.

media:content.media:title: Kennedy will look for opportunities to show the receipts demonstrating the successes of his reforms.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun, Simon J. Levien, Robert King and Cheyenne Haslett
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun, Simon J. Levien, Robert King and Cheyenne Haslett

item.title: Many Americans express doubt about vaccines, POLITICO Poll finds
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s views are commonplace across the land.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Anna Wiederkehr/POLITICO (source images via AP and Getty)
media:content.media:title: MAHA Vaccines

item.dc:creator: By Lauren Gardner

item.title: ‘A crisis in the making’: Nebraska races to impose work requirements on Medicaid
item.description: The state’s May 1 enforcement will be a test case for what could happen nationwide in January.

media:content.media:title: Nebraska Governor Jim Pillen (L) and US Secretary of Agriculture Brooke Rollins (C) listen as US President Donald Trump speaks during a roundtable discussion on rural health care on January 16, 2026. On May 1, Nebraska will become the first state to enforce the Medicaid work requirements Congress enacted last year. (Photo by Brendan SMIALOWSKI / AFP via Getty Images)

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: Your guide to who’s who in the MAHA movement
item.description: Sure, Americans know Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and Mehmet Oz. But Marty Makary? Calley Means?

media:content.media:title: TPP Maha characters lead image

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Anna Wiederkehr
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Anna Wiederkehr

item.title: Trump is still trying to DOGE the NIH. Republicans are tired.
item.description: The NIH has proven a difficult target for the White House budget director, because lawmakers have a symbiotic relationship with the agency.

media:content.media:title: Senate Appropriations Chair Susan Collins (R-Maine) has called the White House's proposed NIH cuts “unwarranted.”

item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper

item.title: RFK Jr. has turned corporate America’s name to mud, POLITICO Poll finds
item.description: Most Americans, especially Republicans, want to regulate food and drug makers now.

media:content.media:title: A majority of both Republicans and Democrats believe food companies are making Americans less healthy for profit, according to POLITICO’s poll.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: After loss in court, RFK Jr. amends guidelines for key vaccine panel to emphasize risks of shots
item.description: A federal judge put the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices on ice last month. The changes could help Kennedy find a way around that decision.

media:content.media:title: Kennedy fired and replaced all the members of the panel last spring.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Lauren Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Lauren Gardner

item.title: Top drug lobbyist to depart PhRMA at year end
item.description: The announcement of Steve Ubl’s departure, disclosed during a board call, caps a decade atop the brand-name drug lobby.

media:content.media:title: Ubl’s announcement leaves a leadership vacuum at one of the most powerful lobbies in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu, David Lim and Lauren Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Amanda Chu, David Lim and Lauren Gardner

item.title: Poll: Here’s what MAHA actually believes
item.description: A new POLITICO Poll finds that Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Make America Healthy again supporters don’t align with him — or each other — on some key issues.

media:content.media:title: TPP: MAHA Beliefs

item.dc:creator: By Marcia Brown and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Marcia Brown and Kelly Hooper
